specification.topic-statement: The PostgreSQL notification subsystem subscribes to and unsubscribes from named channels, sends notifications to channels, and non-blockingly consumes incoming notifications delivered to the connection.

scope.in-scope: LISTEN and UNLISTEN command execution, NOTIFY command execution, connection socket file descriptor access for select()-based monitoring, and non-blocking notification consumption with callback dispatch.
scope.boundary: The underlying PostgreSQL connection is managed by the database connection module. This topic uses that connection but does not manage its lifecycle.

behavior.listen: Subscribes the connection to a named notification channel by executing a LISTEN statement. The channel name is double-quoted in the SQL text. If the query fails, an IO error is returned. On success, the connection will receive notifications published on that channel.
behavior.unlisten: Unsubscribes the connection from a named channel by executing an UNLISTEN statement. Identical error handling to the listen operation. After a successful unlisten, no further notifications from that channel are delivered to the connection.
behavior.notify:
Sends a notification on a named channel with a payload string by executing a NOTIFY statement. The channel name is double-quoted and the payload is single-quoted in the SQL text. If the query fails, an IO error is returned.
The payload is inserted into the SQL string with single quotes but without escaping. A payload containing a single quote or other special SQL characters will produce a malformed query.
behavior.socket-file-descriptor: Returns the integer file descriptor of the underlying PostgreSQL connection socket. This allows the caller to include the connection in a select() or poll() call, enabling event-driven detection of incoming notifications without busy-polling.
behavior.consume-notifications:
Processes all pending notifications on the connection in a non-blocking manner:
1. Reads any available data from the connection socket without blocking. If this read fails, an IO error is returned immediately. 2. Dequeues pending notifications one at a time. For each notification, the caller-supplied callback is invoked with the channel name and payload string as arguments. The notification is freed after the callback returns. 3. Continues until no further notifications are queued.
Returns the count of notifications processed as an unsigned integer in the OK result value.
The consume operation does not block waiting for new notifications. It only processes notifications that have already arrived on the connection socket. Callers must include the connection file descriptor in a select() or equivalent before calling this function to avoid busy-polling.
